title: NHS injury costs recovery scheme: tariff and charges from 1 October 2025
body: "The tariff and ceiling on charges payable by compensators for the recovery of NHS charges under the NHS injury costs recovery (ICR) scheme increased on 1 October 2025. The increases apply only to injuries sustained on or after that date.  This uplift in the level of charges reflects Hospital and Community Health Services (HCHS) inflation. The latest available figure for HCHS inflation is 4.2% from 1 October 2025 to 30 September 2026.  The increased charges are:   where the injured person is provided with NHS ambulance services, the charge is increased from £256 to £267 for each occasion where the injured person receives NHS treatment, but is not admitted to hospital, the charge is increased from £848 to £886 the daily charge for NHS in-patient treatment is increased from £1,042 to £1,085 the maximum charge in respect of an injury is increased from £62,272 to £64,856   The Compensation Recovery Unit will also be issuing a letter to compensators and representatives of the injured person.  For further information, contact:   Injury Costs Recovery policy teamDepartment of Health and Social CareQuarry HouseQuarry HillLeeds LS2 7UE   nhs.injurycostrecovery@dhsc.gov.uk  "
